bno.title: BNO Visa — Absence Calculator
bno.subtitle: Track your absences from the UK to plan for ILR and British citizenship
bno.isLOTR: LOTR (Leave Outside The Rules — applied overseas)
bno.isLOTRHint: LOTR: Your 5-year qualifying period starts from your UK arrival date. No approval date needed, and the pre-arrival gap does not count as absence.
bno.approvalDateHint: If your arrival was within 180 days of visa approval, the 5-year qualifying period starts from the approval date (CR 2.1). Pre-arrival days count as absence.
bno.noTrips: No absences recorded yet. Add your first trip.

ilr.title: ILR Eligibility
ilr.subtitle: Indefinite Leave to Remain (after 5 years)
ilr.clockFromApproval: 5-year clock starts from visa approval date (CR 2.1)
ilr.delayed: ILR application date pushed back — must wait for violations to age out of the 5-year lookback window

citizenship.title: British Citizenship
citizenship.subtitle: Naturalisation (ILR + 1 year)
citizenship.delayed: Application date pushed back due to excess absences
citizenship.todayOverLimitNote: Today's 12-month window exceeds 90 days, but by your application date this window will have reset — it does not affect your citizenship calculation

chart.title: Rolling Absence Chart
chart.subtitle: Full timeline from UK arrival to citizenship eligibility

whatif.title: Simulate a Trip
whatif.desc: Enter planned trip dates to preview the impact on your ILR and citizenship eligibility (not saved to records)
whatif.noImpact: This trip has no impact on your ILR or citizenship eligibility dates
whatif.dateError: Return date must be after departure date

ilr.travelHistoryHint: Compile from passport stamps or export CSV from VisaPath
ilr.lifeInUk: Life in the UK Test pass certificate
ilr.proofEmploymentHint: Employer letter, payslips, or self-employment accounts

citizenship.goodCharacterHint: May require DBS check or overseas police certificate
citizenship.lifeInUk: Life in the UK Test pass certificate

bno.enterArrivalDate: Enter your UK arrival date to begin

csv.importError: Import failed — please check the file format
csv.importWarning: This will overwrite your existing {{n}} records and cannot be undone!
csv.sharePrompt: Copy this link and send to your friend:
csv.shareQrHint: Scan the QR code or copy the link to share
csv.shareReceivedDesc: Your friend shared their trip records with you. Import as a new member?
csv.restoreAllWarning: This will replace all {{current}} member(s) with the {{restore}} member(s) from the backup file. This cannot be undone!

rules.title: Calculation Rules
rules.rule1: Departure and return days are counted as UK days (not absence)
rules.rule2: ILR: No single 12-month rolling period may exceed 180 days absence during the 5-year qualifying period
rules.rule3: Citizenship: Maximum 90 days absent in the 12 months before applying; maximum 450 days in the 5 years before applying
rules.rule4: Based on UK Home Office guidance. This tool does not constitute legal advice.

sw.title: Skilled Worker Visa — Absence Calculator
sw.subtitle: Track absences from the UK to plan for ILR and British citizenship

ilr.lifeInUk: Life in the UK Test pass certificate
ilr.proofEmploymentHint: CoS approval, employer letter, last 3 months payslips

citizenship.lifeInUk: Life in the UK Test pass certificate

familyVisa.title: Family Visa — Absence Calculator
familyVisa.subtitle: Track absences from the UK to plan for ILR and British citizenship (family route)

ilr.lifeInUk: Life in the UK Test pass certificate
ilr.proofEmploymentHint: Marriage certificate, joint account, joint tenancy etc.
ilr.taxReturn: UK sponsor financial evidence (minimum income threshold)

citizenship.lifeInUk: Life in the UK Test pass certificate

hmrc.title: HMRC Statutory Residence Test (SRT)
hmrc.subtitle: Determine your UK tax residence status for the tax year (6 April – 5 April)

section.tiesHint: More ties = lower day threshold to be treated as UK resident

field.ukDaysThisYearHint: Including arrival and departure days (midnight rule: present in UK at midnight counts as 1 day)
field.residentPrior3Years: How many of the prior 3 tax years were you UK resident?
field.residentPrior3YearsHint: 0 = never been UK resident (new arrival); 3 = all three years

tie.familyHint: UK-resident spouse/civil partner or minor child (attending school in UK)
tie.accommodationHint: UK accommodation available ≥91 days this year, and used for at least 1 night
tie.workHint: Worked in UK ≥40 days this year (≥3 hours per day)
tie.ukDaysHint: More days in UK this year than in any other single country
tie.activePriorYearHint: Spent ≥90 days in UK in either of the prior 2 tax years (only applies if previously UK resident)

srt.aot1: Automatic Overseas Test: fewer than 16 UK days — automatically non-resident
srt.aot2: Automatic Overseas Test: fewer than 46 UK days and non-resident all 3 prior years — automatically non-resident
srt.aut1: Automatic UK Test: 183 or more UK days — automatically UK resident

stt.resident: Sufficient Ties Test: UK days exceed the threshold for your tie count — UK resident
stt.nonResident: Sufficient Ties Test: UK days below the threshold for your tie count — non-resident

hmrc.disclaimer: For reference only. Not tax advice. SRT can be complex — consult a qualified tax adviser for your personal situation.

profile.duplicateHint: Copy all records from this member into a new member
profile.deleteConfirm: Delete "{{name}}"? All records will be permanently lost.
profile.backupWarning: All data is stored locally in your browser only — nothing is uploaded to any server. Clearing browser data will permanently delete all records. Please back up regularly.
profile.backupReminder: We recommend backing up first. There is no way to recover deleted data.

crs.title: Canada Express Entry CRS Score Calculator
crs.subtitle: Estimate your Comprehensive Ranking System score based on IRCC's official formula
crs.disclaimer: For estimation purposes only. Actual scores are calculated by IRCC. Results may vary based on individual circumstances.

section.lang1Hint: English or French (IELTS / CELPIP / TEF / TCF — convert to CLB level)
section.lang2Hint: If you have scores in both English and French, you may earn additional points

au.title: Australia Skilled Migration Points Calculator (189/190)
au.subtitle: Calculate your points under the Australian points test — minimum 65 points to pass
au.disclaimer: For estimation purposes only. Actual points are assessed by the Department of Home Affairs. Points schedule may be updated periodically.

other.partnerEnglish: Partner has competent English or above

footer.disclaimer: For reference only. Not legal advice. Always verify with official government sources.
footer.feedback: Report a bug / suggest a feature

ukStudent.title: UK Student Visa
ukStudent.subtitle: Student Route visa requirements checklist
ukStudent.optionalHint: Depends on your subject or nationality
ukStudent.disclaimer: For reference only. Always check UKVI official guidance and consult an immigration adviser.

calc.title: Funding Requirement Calculator
calc.note28days: Funds must be held for 28 consecutive days before application

criteria.cas: Has CAS from licensed Tier 4 / Student sponsor
criteria.casHint: Confirmation of Acceptance for Studies from a UKVI-approved institution
criteria.fundsHint: Calculated from course location, duration and tuition
criteria.englishHint: Must be UKVI-approved test (not standard IELTS)
criteria.passportHint: Passport should remain valid throughout the course
criteria.immigrationHint: No overstays, deception or deportation history
criteria.atasHint: Required for certain postgraduate subjects sensitive to national security
criteria.tbHint: Required for applicants from certain countries (check official list)

canadaStudent.title: Canada Study Permit
canadaStudent.subtitle: Requirements checklist for Canadian study permit
canadaStudent.optionalHint: Depends on your nationality or province
canadaStudent.disclaimer: For reference only. Always verify with IRCC official guidance.

calc.title: Funding Requirement Calculator
calc.formula: Tuition + living expenses ({{living}}/year) + return fare estimate
calc.note: Includes approx. CAD ,000 return fare estimate

criteria.loa: Acceptance letter from a DLI (Designated Learning Institution)
criteria.loaHint: Must be from a IRCC-approved Designated Learning Institution
criteria.passportHint: Must be valid for the duration of study
criteria.intent: Intend to leave Canada after studies
criteria.intentHint: Must demonstrate genuine temporary entrant intention
criteria.languageHint: May be waived if instruction is in English or French
criteria.medicalHint: Required for applicants from certain countries or studying health-related programs
criteria.biometricsHint: Most applicants need to provide biometrics

auStudent.title: Australia Student Visa (Subclass 500)
auStudent.subtitle: Requirements checklist for Australian student visa
auStudent.disclaimer: For reference only. Always check Home Affairs official guidance.

criteria.cricos: Enrolled with a CRICOS registered provider
criteria.cricosHint: Commonwealth Register of Institutions and Courses for Overseas Students
criteria.oshcHint: Health insurance mandatory for the duration of study
criteria.englishHint: Minimum varies by institution and course level
criteria.genuineHint: Must show genuine intention to study, not to migrate permanently

cs.title: Ireland Critical Skills Employment Permit
cs.subtitle: Eligibility checker for Irish CSEP
cs.onCsol: Occupation is on the Critical Skills Occupations List (CSOL)
cs.onCsolHint: Check the DETE Critical Skills Occupations List
cs.thresholdA: Salary must be ≥ {{amount}} for CSOL occupations
cs.thresholdB: Salary must be ≥ {{amount}} for non-CSOL pathway
cs.pathwayADesc: Occupation on CSOL + salary ≥ {{amount}} + degree or 5 years experience
cs.pathwayBDesc: Any occupation (not excluded) + salary ≥ {{amount}} + degree or experience

criteria.jobOffer: Has a job offer from an Irish employer
criteria.jobOfferHint: Must be a genuine job offer with a contract
criteria.qualification: Has Level 8+ degree OR 5+ years relevant experience
criteria.qualificationHint: NFQ Level 8 (Honours degree) or equivalent international qualification
criteria.employer: Employer is registered with Revenue Commissioners
criteria.employerHint: Employer must be tax-compliant and registered in Ireland

cs.disclaimer: For reference only. CSOL and salary thresholds may change. Check DETE official website.

stamp4.title: Ireland Stamp 4 Eligibility
stamp4.subtitle: Check your path to long-term residence in Ireland
stamp4.eligibleDesc: You have met the residence requirement. Ensure other criteria are also met.
stamp4.immediateDesc: As spouse/partner of a Critical Skills EP holder, you can apply straight away.
stamp4.otherAdvice: Please consult an immigration adviser to determine your path to Stamp 4.
stamp4.startDateLabel: Date you received your current permission

status.spouseCritical: Spouse / partner of Critical Skills EP holder

statusHint.criticalSkills: Working in Ireland on a Critical Skills EP
statusHint.generalPermit: Working in Ireland on a General Work Permit
statusHint.spouseCritical: Your spouse/partner holds a Critical Skills EP
statusHint.stamp1g: Graduated from an Irish institution, currently on job-seeking permission
statusHint.other: Including students, refugees, family reunification, etc.

criteria.continuousHint: No gaps or periods of unlawful stay
criteria.goodStanding: No criminal convictions and in good standing

stamp4.disclaimer: For reference only. Immigration rules change frequently. Always verify with INIS or consult an adviser.

au186.title: Australia Employer Nomination Scheme (Subclass 186)
au186.subtitle: Permanent residence through employer sponsorship

streamDesc.trt: Already working for your nominating employer in Australia for 3+ years on TSS/457
streamDesc.de: Applying from overseas or with less than 3 years with the employer

trt.inAustraliaHint: Must be onshore to apply in this stream
trt.threeYears: Worked for nominating employer for 3+ years full-time
trt.tssDollarHint: Same occupation as on TSS visa
trt.under45Hint: Some exemptions apply for certain occupations
trt.englishHint: Some exemptions apply for passport holders of certain countries

de.skillsAssessment: Positive skills assessment from relevant authority
de.skillsAssessmentHint: Must be assessed by the relevant Australian skills assessment body
de.employerNomination: Employer nomination approved by Home Affairs
de.employerNominationHint: Employer must be approved and apply for nomination first
de.pointsNote: Points Test Required for DE Stream
de.pointsDesc: You must score 65+ points in the SkillSelect points test. Use the Australia Points Calculator to check your score.

au186.disclaimer: For reference only. 186 visa requirements are complex. Always consult a registered migration agent.

pnp.title: Canada Provincial Nominee Program (PNP)
pnp.subtitle: Check eligibility for provincial immigration streams
pnp.benefit: PNP adds 600 points to your CRS score
pnp.benefitDesc: A provincial nomination adds 600 points to your Express Entry CRS score, almost guaranteeing an invitation to apply (ITA).
pnp.inExpressEntryHint: Have an active Express Entry profile (CRS score submitted)

bc.title: BC PNP – Skills Immigration
bc.jobOffer: Has a job offer from a BC employer
bc.jobOfferHint: Full-time, indeterminate (permanent) or for the duration of the project
bc.nocABHint: Most professional, technical and skilled trade occupations qualify
bc.workAuth: Currently authorized to work in BC (or in Express Entry)
bc.workAuthHint: Valid work permit in BC, or active Express Entry profile (BCPNP Tech Pilot)
bc.note: BC PNP also has the Tech Pilot stream for tech occupations — requires Express Entry profile and job offer in eligible tech occupation.

ontario.title: OINP – Ontario Immigrant Nominee Program
ontario.jobOffer: Has a job offer from an Ontario employer
ontario.techOccupation: Occupation is in the OINP eligible tech list
ontario.techOccupationHint: Tech Draw targets specific NOC codes in tech sector
ontario.note: OINP draws are unpredictable. The Human Capital Priority stream selects directly from Express Entry pool without requiring job offer.

alberta.title: AAIP – Alberta Advantage Immigration Program
alberta.jobOffer: Has a job offer from an Alberta employer
alberta.eeStream: In Express Entry pool AND meets Alberta Express Entry stream criteria
alberta.eeStreamHint: Alberta EE stream selects candidates with Alberta connection
alberta.note: Alberta Opportunity Stream requires applicants already working in Alberta with a valid work permit.

sask.title: SINP – Saskatchewan Immigrant Nominee Program
sask.jobOffer: Has a job offer from a Saskatchewan employer
sask.experience: Has relevant work experience in nominated occupation
sask.experienceHint: Minimum 1 year of work experience in the nominated occupation

other.info: Other provinces and territories (Manitoba, Nova Scotia, New Brunswick, PEI, NL, YT, NT, NU) also have PNP programs. Each has unique streams and requirements. Visit the official IRCC website for details on all provincial programs.

pnp.disclaimer: PNP rules change frequently. Always check the official provincial immigration website and IRCC for current requirements.

rnip.title: Rural & Northern Immigration Pilot (RNIP)
rnip.subtitle: Check your eligibility and find a participating community
rnip.statusDesc: The RNIP program was set to expire in December 2024. It may have been extended or replaced. Please verify current status on the official IRCC website before applying.
rnip.hasJobOfferNote: Job offer strengthens your community recommendation!

field.ecaHint: Issued by a designated body (e.g. WES, ICAS)
field.fundsHint: Based on family size; similar to Express Entry requirements
field.jobOffer: Has a job offer from a participating community employer
field.jobOfferHint: Full-time, non-seasonal job offer is a strong advantage
field.communityLinkHint: Having a connection to a community strengthens your application

rnip.expRequirement: Minimum 1 year (1,560 hours) of continuous full-time work experience required
rnip.langRequirement: Your NOC level requires CLB {{min}} or higher
rnip.ecaNote: ECA required for post-secondary education obtained outside Canada

noc.CD: NOC C / D – Semi-skilled & Entry Level

rnip.communityNote: Each community sets its own criteria and intake rounds. Contact communities directly for current openings.
rnip.step1: Identify 1–3 communities that match your skills and job opportunities
rnip.step2: Contact the community immigration representative directly
rnip.step3: Apply to the community and receive a recommendation letter
rnip.step4: Submit PR application to IRCC with the community recommendation
rnip.disclaimer: This tool is for reference only. RNIP program details and community participation may change. Always verify current information on the IRCC official website.
